summary: Surah Saba highlights Allah’s omniscience, the certainty of resurrection and judgment, and the consequences of gratitude and ingratitude. It recounts the stories of Dawud, Sulaiman, and the people of Saba, illustrating divine favor and punishment. The surah refutes idolatry, emphasizes the futility of relying on wealth or status, and stresses that only faith and good deeds bring nearness to Allah. It warns against arrogance and denial, and affirms the universality of the Prophet’s message.
